title: COLLEGE MOVE IN VLOG *freshman year* @BaylorUniversity | welcome week, dorm organization & friends
body:
Hi, I’m only a sophomore (about to be a junior) in high school but I was trying to look into colleges. Currently, Baylor is my first choice but I want to make sure the school can lead me into the right area when it comes to my future career choice. I plan on becoming a pediatrician, but I’m not sure where to start. Do you have any advice or tips?

Also, I want to continue my passion on the flute, but I’m worried about the time management. How do you balance your life between your instrument and nutritional sciences?

body:
Dallas is only 1.5 hours away and a perfect 2-3 day getaway for some flashy restaurants and bigtime shopping.

I went to UCLA with all of Los Angeles at my fingertips. My senior year when i desperately needed money for my senior project at the film school, i ended up working at an exclusive but not very Godly bar and it lead me down a bad path for several years.

I am helping a friend decide on college and my vote at this point is Baylor!

title: baylor university pros and cons 💚💛 a student's perspective on housing, campus culture, waco, + more!
body:
Hello! Thank you for making this video - it's really nice to see a student's perspective on Baylor.

Personally, I am an incoming college freshman and Baylor is my top choice. As someone who is not Christian, it does scare me a bit to attend because I do not want to be excluded.

Do you think religion is a big aspect in attending Baylor? I do not mind that, but I just do not want to be left out from things because of my religion.

title: baylor university pros and cons 💚💛 a student's perspective on housing, campus culture, waco, + more!
body:
Hi Sophie!! Thank you for this very helpful video!

I’m a junior in high school and would love to be a part of University Scholars at Baylor because I believe it can help me go deeper into subjects that I love to study (religion, and classics)!

Although I don’t think I have a good chance of getting in because of my stats from my first two years. If I don’t make it in University Scholars before the Fall of my freshman year, am I allowed to apply again? Also, if I apply after the Fall of my freshman year of college will they look at my academic history from high school, Fall of freshman year, or both?

Go Bears!!🐻🟡🟢 Sic ‘em!! 🐾

title: Baylor University: Likes & Dislikes
body: God bless you!  While I don't know you personally, I am extremely proud of you!  You are the voice that is urgently needed.  I always talk to my daughters about how many need to have more "spine" and not be easily influenced by other people and organizations with questionable agendas. title: Baylor University: Likes & Dislikes
body: I normally don't write comments on videos but we just visited Baylor last week with my family. We're from SoCal and we visited the campus with my daughters and we didn't know what to expect. The school exceeded our expectations. I appreciate your pros and cons and wish the university would take a stand on Biblical views and not cave-in to societal pressures. Sounds like there are some solid students there though and the size of the student body makes the school seem very appealing. You are also very well-spoken and intelligent. It is clear that you could have gone to other great schools but you chose Baylor which will help attract other students of your caliber after they see your videos!

title: Baylor University: Likes & Dislikes
body: 100% agree with the whole video, especially the discrepancy between the loud minority and the silent majority. Like you said, they’ve made it very hard for the majority to speak out and be as free as the minority has been to voice their opinions. Also, in regards to dorm life, I would definitely say to any incoming freshman that your only friends should not be within your dorm. A lot of drama goes down and it’s hard to escape it, unfortunately. Branch out and meet other people in other dorms; diversify your friend group. Loved the whole thing though, keep up the great work! Representing Baylor well💯🙌🏼🐻

title: Day In The Life: D1 Athlete Vs Frat Student
body: And this is why I hate the phrase bitter people throw around “oh it must be nice to just get paid millions to catch a ball” these dudes grind their asses off & dedicate their lives to this sport, not including all the physical & mental abuse toll football takes. They chose their line of work, & so did you lol
author: @KBarron43

title: Day In The Life: D1 Athlete Vs Frat Student
body:
D1 sports students are just pure respect.
I’m a cs major and people say “oh it must be so hard” or “oh it’s gotta be tiring” nah, compared to sports students it’s easy other than 1 semester. It would’ve been hilarious to see a realistic cs student compared with an athlete. Cause trust me, most of the “day in the life of a cs student” videos are just trying to appeal to the stereotype of a cs student being all studious, when in reality most of us see a B as a reason to party lol

title: Day In The Life: D1 Athlete Vs Frat Student

title: Day In The Life: D1 Athlete Vs Frat Student
body: Man I miss this so much man. I had to medically retire due to how many severe concussions I’ve had before my college career could really get started. I pray I’ll be able to return to the game and show all the hard work I put in got me to become but I don’t know man.
author: @RyderMariani-Shorts

title: Day In The Life: D1 Athlete Vs Frat Student
body:
Man it can be so tempting especially with social media, but this vid puts it right in perspective. While he’s out grinding it’s hard. It’s real real hard. Specially when u check in stories on sc seeing all the social events ur missing out on.
It’s even confusing, you’ll find urself asking over and over “is this grind worth it?” It’s Mental game of endurance not just to do it but to stay committed to choosing that life over the alternative.

In the end tho (for those that don’t go NFL) you’re both walking away with a degree from the same university, but that level of self discipline the D1 player has will carry him to be respected in everything he/she does
author: @SowaScape_Premier_Landscapes

title: Day In The Life: D1 Athlete Vs Frat Student
body: Not gonna lie… the frat life was more fun.  I played football for 7 years, finishing at a 5A Texas high school program…. and even 5A high school programs take it so seriously that’s it’s a full time way of life.   Chose not to play college ball, to be a regular student and join a frat instead.  Gotta say, life became much more enjoyable after that
author: @DavidSellsDenver

title: Day In The Life: D1 Athlete Vs Frat Student

title: Day In The Life: D1 Athlete Vs Frat Student
body: One thing not mentioned is the difference in degrees that you see on the football team vs a fraternity. When I was in college most of us who were active members got degrees in finance, business, engineering, biology, etc. I could never figure Out how some of the football guys got good degrees like that while playing ball. My last 2 years of school all I did was work and study. It was a grind

author: @rutha2403

title: Day in the Life of a COLLEGE STUDENT! | University Freshman Daily Routine
body: You probably won't see this, but in case you do; please realise that you have a very big social life compared to most people. This means that not every fresher is having this much fun or immediately connects with the right people during the first few weeks. I, myself, am in my final year of college and only felt like I truly belonged to a good friendgroup at the start of the second year. So also for those of you who are watching this and are feeling like you do not (yet) have this many friends: that is okay. You are okay. Sometimes it takes time to settle. And maybe you are in a friendgroup now but you will be in a different one in a few months and that is all okay. Just stay open-minded and don't be too hard on yourself. Good luck, you can do this!
author: @sannelouters3005

title: Day in the Life of a COLLEGE STUDENT! | University Freshman Daily Routine
body: 1:30am is when college students start going to bed? Oh, Brooklyn, honey. Excuse me while I go laugh all the way to 4am crying about homework and exams lol (this is coming from a senior in college btw)
author: @softchai

title: Day in the Life of a COLLEGE STUDENT! | University Freshman Daily Routine
body: Don’t be fooled kids. This isn’t how college is. You sleep all day. Wear sweatpants with over sized tees. And do homework the night before it’s due
author: @Melaniesmakeupbag

title: Day in the Life of a COLLEGE STUDENT! | University Freshman Daily Routine
body: My first year of college was a lot like theirs so idk why everyone is saying this isn’t realistic. Like some of us really do wake up early, put in effort into how we look, and don’t procrastinate so horribly. The only thing I couldn’t relate to was staying up late because I prioritized sleep.
author: @marywilder84

title: Day in the Life of a COLLEGE STUDENT! | University Freshman Daily Routine
body: Lol y’all have been in college for a few weeks and you talk like you’ve been in college for years😂😂
author: @sanjanadandu5959

title: Day in the Life of a COLLEGE STUDENT! | University Freshman Daily Routine
body: To everyone who is saying that the twins are saying “this is the college kid life” too much (or something along those lines), try to understand that there’s a lot of smaller clips taken at different times. To them, it might seem like they’ve only said the phrase a few times (in each clip), but to us, when we watched the whole video, the phrases added up to a lot. You guys also have to understand they can’t just cut that part out or they’d be losing a larger portion of the video. They say the phrases in the middle of a lot of stuff where it can’t be cut out.
author: @rachelzhao6109

title: Day in the Life of a COLLEGE STUDENT! | University Freshman Daily Routine
body: Just wait till y’all get later in your freshmen year, you’re gonna be rolling out of bed at the last minute throwing on some sweats and going to class with no make up 😂 also get used to the no sleep and busy schedules!! - this is coming from a college senior

author: @dellojollo255

title: We Joined a SORORITY! | Can You Guess Which One?
body: Fair warning. The sorority process is not all smiles and  giggles. It can be hard looking at how “happy” girls who rush and join are. At least it’s hard for me.  It can fool you into thinking “hey. Those girls look happy. I want to be that happy. So maybe i should join a sorority like them and i can be happy too”. It doesn’t always workout like that. The rush process is stressful and can hype you up to a let down. The most common example is your favorite house dropping you which leads you to question yourself and wonder “why am i not good enough to get into XYZ sorority” which brings you down a path of tearing yourself apart. Then there’s sorority rankings which can sway your decision. Idk. Just fair warning. The sorority process is a rollercoaster. Don’t expect “all good times all the time”
author: @andrianachin3914

title: We Joined a SORORITY! | Can You Guess Which One?
body: “Wow this is dumb at my college we actually learn” I’m sure I’m not the first person to say this but it’s not impossible to focus on school and a club at the same time. Sorority women have some of the highest GPAs at their schools because of the academic requirements they have. And I promise not every sorority woman is shallow and dumb. If that’s all you know, you know the wrong women. Just let other people enjoy themselves. I don’t even know who they are I just clicked on the video bc I was curious which one they joined 🤷🏼‍♀️

title: We Joined a SORORITY! | Can You Guess Which One?
body: For everyone asking what a sorority is, it is basically a large organization that raises money for a specific philanthropy (or charity). I am not sure what Chi Omega's is, but I will use mine for example. I was a Delta Gamma and our philanthropy is Service for Sight. So, any women who is a Delta Gamma helped raise money to help those who had visual impairments (blind, glasses, etc.). Although our main purpose is to raise money for our philanthropy, there were a lot of other fun aspects to being in a sorority. We had socials (with fraternities), date parties and formals (like college prom or homecoming). We could hold leadership positions (which helped us gain experience in a leadership role). If you are a female you can join a sorority and if you are a male you can join a fraternity (all together you guys are called Greek Life).  Although being in a sorority isn't for everyone, there are a lot of great benefits from it. Hopefully this helps! Feel free to ask any further questions about it
author: @bcd3496
